summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting on September 9, 2025 with Trustee Allen absent. Key topics included a public presentation by Rachel Walker about hosting vendor markets and festivals on her 40-acre property on Kerbet Road, a presentation from the Clermont County Mental Health and Recovery Board about their renewal levy on the November ballot, and several financial and administrative motions. The board also went into executive session to discuss public employee matters.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (August 27, 2025 through September 9, 2025) – passed
Motion to increase appropriations in drug fund motor vehicles (22217607500000) in the amount of $17,904.93 – passed
Motion to accept donation of three semis and one semi trailer from City Service for heavy rescue training and approve disposal via scrap upon completion – passed
Motion to accept the 2024 OBM ARPA Ohio Ambulance Retention Grant in the amount of $41,761 retroactively to 7-16-2024 – passed
Motion to renew lease with Quadrant for postage machine – passed
Motion to adjourn into Executive Session at 6:21 PM – passed
Motion to adjourn the meeting at 7:11 PM – passed
action_items: Planning and Zoning Director to contact Rachel Walker regarding commercial conditional use permit process for vendor markets/festivals on Kerbet Road property
Rachel Walker to present project to BZA at the October meeting
Chief Myers to obtain titles for cement truck and finalize arrangements for heavy rescue training class at end of September
Chief Myers to take photos of heavy rescue training class
key_amounts: $17,904.93 – increase in appropriations in drug fund motor vehicles from equitable sharing (DEA) funds
$41,761 – 2024 OBM ARPA Ohio Ambulance Retention Grant accepted retroactively
$73.12/month – renewed postage machine lease rate (down from $73.26)
$13.04 (corrected from $35.04) – estimated annual increase in postage costs due to rate increase
$3.3 million/year – revenue generated by Clermont County Mental Health and Recovery Board renewal levy
$15 per $100,000 valuation – cost of Mental Health and Recovery Board renewal levy
0.75 mills – Mental Health and Recovery Board renewal levy rate
notable_topics: Rachel Walker's proposal for vintage artisan vendor markets and family-friendly festivals on 40-acre Kerbet Road property
Clermont County Mental Health and Recovery Board renewal levy presentation for November ballot
Station 18 construction update – approximately two weeks ahead of schedule
Zoning workshop discussion on increasing minimum lot size in PUD restrictions and distance between houses
Heavy rescue training class using donated semi trucks from City Service
Drug fund appropriation transfer from equitable sharing/DEA funds
ARPA Ambulance Retention Grant acceptance for audit compliance
Executive session regarding public employee appointment, employment, discipline, or compensation
attendees: Clermont County Mental Health and Recovery Board representative
notes: Trustee Allen was absent and noted to be traveling. The transcript contains several sections of garbled or repeated text likely due to automated transcription errors. The zoning workshop held the previous night discussed increasing PUD lot size minimums and setback requirements. A moratorium on single-family home development is currently in effect. The heavy rescue training class is described as an annual event held on the green property across from a township facility. The Walker property was previously approved for approximately 112 homes but the Walkers intend a different agricultural/commercial use instead.
title: BOT 9-9-25
